title: heuchera micrantha var. erubescens
intro: Heuchera micrantha var. erubescens, also known as Coral Bells, is a variety of the Heuchera micrantha plant, which is a type of flowering perennial native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The medicinal properties of Coral Bells include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, soothe coughs, and promote wound healing. Coral Bells can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves for its medicinal uses, as well as used in culinary dishes for its unique flavor and nutritional value.
